design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate through seven stages shooting airborne enemies, dropping bombs on ground targets, collecting color-changing bell power-ups, and defeating stage bosses. Core mechanic involves shooting bells mid-air to change their color before collection to trigger different upgrades.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing health via life meter system (replacing traditional lives) while navigating bullet patterns and enemy formations across seven escalating stages.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a cute anthropomorphic aircraft (TwinBee or WinBee) to save the world from a mad scientist's army, with cooperative multiplayer fantasy of two pilots working together.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes accessibility and social play through 'couple mode' (reduces enemy targeting toward less-experienced Player 2), shared life energy mechanics, and the ability to use your partner's ship as a weapon. Trades traditional arcade harshness for approachability while maintaining shmup DNA. Quirky, light-hearted aesthetic (idol-influenced soundtrack, character-driven narrative) positions it as 'cute shmup' rather than austere arcade shooter.

difficulty.lives_system: Replaced by shared life meter; damage accumulates until meter depletes, triggering stage restart.
difficulty.continues_system: Limited continues available after losing; player restarts at the stage where they left off.
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpoint system; continues restart player at their last stage attempt.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Adjustable difficulty settings in options menu. 'Couple mode' explicitly reduces targeting of Player 2 to enable skill-leveled cooperative play. Players can choose companion ship formation type (follow, engulf, or surround) affecting tactical gameplay.

progression.upgrade_system: Color-coded bells serve as primary power-up system. Shooting bells in mid-air changes their color, with each color granting different upgrades upon collection. Companion ship stocks limit special attack usage (tiny ship replicas).
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven stages presented sequentially; progression is gate-locked by boss defeats at stage conclusions.
replayability_hooks: Adjustable difficulty settings encourage replays at higher challenge levels
Multiplayer cooperation introduces emergent tactics and shared life management strategy
Bell color system rewards skill in color-cycling for optimal power-ups
Couple mode couples less-experienced players with veterans, encouraging social replay

world_building_techniques: Opening cutscene establishes plot: TwinBee and WinBee receive distress signal from Madoka, granddaughter of Dr. Mardock (benevolent scientist turned mad after head injury, now seeks world conquest with Acorn Men army)
Character naming system (player enters aircraft name at start) personalizes narrative framing
Cute character design (TwinBee blue, WinBee pink, anthropomorphic aircraft) establishes whimsical world tone reinforced by idol-influenced soundtrack

boss_design.philosophy: One boss per stage (seven total) designed to punctuate linear progression. Bosses function as narrative culmination points (confrontation with Dr. Mardock's forces) rather than pattern-memorization skill checks.

faction_indicators: Player 1 (TwinBee, blue) vs Player 2 (WinBee, pink) partnership framing
Couple mode mechanic explicitly supports skill-disparate partnerships (experienced vs. inexperienced)
danger_signals: Dr. Mardock's insanity-driven world conquest plot
Army of Acorn Men as opposing force
Distress signal from Madoka establishes urgency and threat to innocents
environmental_storytelling: Donburi Island setting provides geographical anchoring for narrative
Seven stages likely represent different zones/territories under Dr. Mardock control, progressing toward final confrontation
Companion ship choice (follow/engulf/surround formation) personalizes aesthetic interaction with world
